sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Low hemoglobin levels and fatigue.
History of Present Illness
Stephanie is a 49-year-old female with a history of congestive heart failure, kidney stones, and a prior colonoscopy. She presents today with complaints of fatigue and dizziness over the past couple of months, noting that these symptoms have worsened recently, particularly with travel. She reports increased swelling in her ankles and has been struggling to maintain a low-salt diet. She had a colonoscopy three years ago that revealed mild diverticulosis with no further issues. She also had kidney stones a couple of years ago, with no recent flares. She reports occasional nasal congestion related to seasonal allergies but no other significant symptoms, including fever, chills, sore throat, or cough. She denies any weight loss or loss of appetite and has not experienced any episodes of fainting or dark stools.
Findings
Physical exam reveals blood pressure 128/78 mmHg, heart rate 72 bpm, and trace 1+ pitting edema in the ankles. A three out of six systolic ejection murmur is heard. The echocardiogram shows a left ventricular ejection fraction of 45% and moderate mitral regurgitation. Hemoglobin is 8.2 g/dL.
Assessment
The patient presents with anemia, likely secondary to chronic disease and lifestyle factors. Her congestive heart failure and recent travel contribute to her symptoms.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I am ordering an anemia profile to determine the etiology of the anemia. I will also refer her back to the gastroenterologist for an endoscopy and repeat colonoscopy. I will initiate lasix 40 mg daily, continue toprol 50 mg daily, and lisinopril 10 mg daily. I will also order a referral to gastroenterology for further evaluation. I will instruct the patient to monitor her weight daily and call if there is any significant increase.
